channel.title: NPR Programs: Morning Edition
channel.description: Morning Edition takes listeners around the country and the world with multi-faceted stories and commentaries every weekday. Hosts Steve Inskeep, Leila Fadel, Michel Martin and A Martínez bring you the latest breaking news and features to prepare you for the day.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Primaries in 4 states set several high-profile matchups for general election
item.description: Voters in four states went to the polls Tuesday, selecting contenders for several high-profile races in November. A look at the results.
item.content:encoded: Voters in four states went to the polls Tuesday, selecting contenders for several high-profile races in November. A look at the results.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: Analyst breaks down the results of Tuesday's key primaries
item.description: NPR's Michel Martin sizes up the outcome of the key Tuesday primary races with Cook Political Report elections analyst Dave Wasserman.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Michel Martin sizes up the outcome of the key Tuesday primary races with Cook Political Report elections analyst Dave Wasserman.
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin

item.title: Bill Gates tells lawmakers he was not aware of Epstein's crimes
item.description: Gates is sitting for a closed-door interview before the House Oversight Committee about his relationship with convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein.
item.content:encoded:
Gates is sitting for a closed-door interview before the House Oversight Committee about his relationship with convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein.

(Image credit: Tom Brenner)
item.dc:creator: Ava Berger

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: U.S. and Iran exchange strikes after Apache helicopter is downed, takeaways from Tuesday's primaries in four states, House Republicans approve a bill to fund ICE for the remainder of Trump's term.
item.content:encoded: U.S. and Iran exchange strikes after Apache helicopter is downed, takeaways from Tuesday's primaries in four states, House Republicans approve a bill to fund ICE for the remainder of Trump's term.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: Soundgarden guitarist Kim Thayil chronicles the band's ascent in his new memoir
item.description: Soundgarden guitarist Kim Thayil writes about the ascent of the first grunge band to sign with a major label and the death of lead singer Chris Cornell in his memoir, "A Screaming Life."
item.content:encoded: Soundgarden guitarist Kim Thayil writes about the ascent of the first grunge band to sign with a major label and the death of lead singer Chris Cornell in his memoir, "A Screaming Life."
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: China and North Korea renew ties, complicating U.S. response to Kim's nuclear program
item.description: With Xi Jinping and Kim Jong Un reaffirming ties while sidestepping nuclear tensions, the U.S. faces a growing challenge in responding to an increasingly confident, nuclear-armed North Korea.
item.content:encoded: With Xi Jinping and Kim Jong Un reaffirming ties while sidestepping nuclear tensions, the U.S. faces a growing challenge in responding to an increasingly confident, nuclear-armed North Korea.
item.dc:creator: Anthony Kuhn

item.title: World Cup fever sweeps Canada, but will it last beyond kickoff?
item.description: World Cup fever is hitting Canada ahead of kickoff Thursday. With soaring ticket prices and growing questions off the pitch, will passion for the game last?
item.content:encoded: World Cup fever is hitting Canada ahead of kickoff Thursday. With soaring ticket prices and growing questions off the pitch, will passion for the game last?
item.dc:creator: Sheena Rossiter

item.title: Nearly half of U.S. families couldn't afford basic necessities in 2024, report finds
item.description: A new report finds close to half of American families did not earn enough to cover necessities in 2024. And with prices continuing to rise, there's little sign that's changed.
item.content:encoded: A new report finds close to half of American families did not earn enough to cover necessities in 2024. And with prices continuing to rise, there's little sign that's changed.
item.dc:creator: Stephan Bisaha

item.title: SCOTUS decision on mail-in ballots could affect states where weather can cause delays
item.description: The Supreme Court is considering overturning a Mississippi law that allows counting of mail-in ballots that arrive after Election Day. That will affect Alaska, where ballots can be weather-delayed.
item.content:encoded: The Supreme Court is considering overturning a Mississippi law that allows counting of mail-in ballots that arrive after Election Day. That will affect Alaska, where ballots can be weather-delayed.
item.dc:creator: Samantha Watson

item.title: Former Middle East adviser on the U.S. launching retaliatory strikes on Iran
item.description: The U.S. military said it launched strikes on Iran for downing an Apache helicopter. NPR's Michel Martin speaks to Michael Singh, a former Middle East adviser in the George W. Bush White House.
item.content:encoded: The U.S. military said it launched strikes on Iran for downing an Apache helicopter. NPR's Michel Martin speaks to Michael Singh, a former Middle East adviser in the George W. Bush White House.
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin
